steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: many mushrooms contain phenolic compounds like cinnamic acid and catechin. Sentence 2: gives specific cases — cinnamic acid in one mushroom, catechin in another.

steps.title: Pick the matching transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is example. The first sentence makes a general claim (many mushrooms contain phenolic compounds such as cinnamic acid and catechin); the second gives specific detected instances of those same compounds in named mushroom species. The blank needs a transition signaling that a specific example follows.
  • Choice A — "For this reason,": signals cause-effect — the second sentence would be a result of the first. Detecting cinnamic acid in a specific mushroom is not caused by the general claim; it is an illustration of it. ✗
  • Choice B — "However,": signals contrast — the second sentence would push back against the first. Confirming specific compounds in specific mushrooms supports the general claim, not contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice C — "Nevertheless,": also signals contrast despite an obstacle. Same problem as "However" — no contradiction exists. ✗
  • Choice D — "For example,": signals that a specific instance of the general claim follows, fitting perfectly since the second sentence names exactly which compounds appear in which mushrooms. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
